assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md, the Parsed CVE Data table correctly lists: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, Vulnerable library = quinn-proto, Affected version range = 'versions before 0.11.14 (< 0.11.14)', Fixed version = 0.11.14. All four data points match the vulnerability issue fixture.

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md, the Ecosystem Detection section states: 'The vulnerable library quinn-proto is a Rust crate. Based on the Ecosystem Mappings table in the 2.2.x stream's security-matrix.md, the ecosystem is Cargo, with: Lock File: Cargo.lock, Check Command: git show <tag>:Cargo.lock, Upstream Branch: release/0.4.z, Repository: backend'.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, the Version Impact Table includes all 7 versions from both streams in security-matrix-mock.md: 2.1.0 (v0.3.8), 2.1.1 (v0.3.12), 2.2.0 (v0.4.5), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8), 2.2.2 (v0.4.9), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11), 2.2.4 (v0.4.12). The security-matrix-mock.md supportability matrices list exactly these 7 versions (2 in 2.1.x stream, 5 in 2.2.x stream). No versions are skipped.

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, the Source Tag column shows pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix: v0.3.8, v0.3.12, v0.4.5, v0.4.8, v0.4.9, v0.4.11, v0.4.12 — matching exactly the backend column values from security-matrix-mock.md. The analysis does not use branch HEAD; it uses the per-version pinned tags. The affects-versions.md further confirms: 'RHTPA 2.2.0 (v0.4.5): quinn-proto 0.11.9', 'RHTPA 2.2.1 (v0.4.8): quinn-proto 0.11.12' etc., all referencing specific pinned tags.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, version 2.2.2 row shows: Source Tag = v0.4.9, quinn-proto = '--', Affected? = YES, Notes = 'retag of 2.2.1 (same source commit v0.4.8)'. This correctly identifies 2.2.2 as a retag and carries forward the YES affected status from 2.2.1. The security-matrix-mock.md confirms v0.4.9 is a retag of v0.4.8 with Note 'backend retag of 2.2.1'.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md, the Proposed Correction section shows a clear diff: 'Current: [RHTPA 2.0.0]' -> 'Proposed: [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]'. The rationale explains: 'PSIRT assigned RHTPA 2.0.0 which does not correspond to any configured version stream... Lock file analysis at pinned source commits from security-matrix.md shows that quinn-proto < 0.11.14 is present in versions 2.2.0 (v0.4.5: 0.11.9), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8: 0.11.12), and 2.2.2 (retag of 2.2.1).' The proposed comment also documents the replacement with evidence.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md, the Proposed Correction uses version names: 'RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2'. The Proposed Jira Mutation section explicitly uses placeholder IDs: '{"id": "<RHTPA-2.2.0-jira-id>"}' etc., with a note: 'Jira version IDs would be discovered dynamically via getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields (Step 3.1). The IDs shown above are placeholders -- actual IDs must be resolved at runtime.' This confirms version names from the matrix are used rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence:
In remediation.md, Task 1 (upstream backport) description includes: '## Repository
backend', '## Target Branch
release/0.4.z', '## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] quinn-proto dependency is >= 0.11.14
- [ ] No other dependency conflicts introduced
- [ ] Existing tests pass'. Task 2 (downstream propagation) includes: '## Repository
rhtpa-release.0.4.z', '## Target Branch
main', '## Acceptance Criteria'. Linkage section shows: 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: <upstream-task-key>, outwardIssue: <downstream-task-key>, type: "Blocks")' establishing dependency between upstream and downstream tasks.

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Case A creates exactly two tasks for stream 2.2.x: 'Task 1: Upstream Backport Task (2.2.x)' with summary 'Remediate CVE-2026-31812: bump quinn-proto to 0.11.14 (2.2.x)' targeting release/0.4.z, and 'Task 2: Downstream Propagation Subtask (2.2.x)' with summary 'Propagate CVE-2026-31812 fix: update backend ref in rhtpa-release.0.4.z (2.2.x)'. The Linkage section explicitly creates a Blocks dependency: 'PROPOSAL: jira.create_link(inwardIssue: <upstream-task-key>, outwardIssue: <downstream-task-key>, type: "Blocks")'.

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: Throughout all output files, actions are consistently framed as proposals: affects-versions.md uses 'PROPOSAL (requires engineer confirmation before execution)' and 'PROPOSAL: jira.edit_issue(...)' and 'PROPOSAL: jira.add_comment(...)'. remediation.md uses 'PROPOSAL: Create Jira Task', 'PROPOSAL: jira.create_issue(...)', 'PROPOSAL: jira.create_link(...)', 'PROPOSAL: Add ai-cve-triaged label to TC-8001', 'PROPOSAL: Transition TC-8001 to In Progress status', and 'All proposed Jira mutations require engineer confirmation before execution.' No output indicates that any Jira mutation was actually executed.
